content_of_post_to_comment: Grief sits in me today. The deep heavy kind. I needed to lie down and process it a little. Grief for my family who can’t see me clearly, grief for the times I continue to lack support sometimes, grief for having to move away from some support to free myself from control, grief for the friends I left behind, grief for not knowing how my future will go, or if my previously imagined futures will pan out how I once hoped. Grief for this entire world. For the loneliness we all encounter. Grief for the crying planet and the people who wish for freedom. All of it. I am letting it be. It isn’t easy. I want to shut the door on it. Kick it out of my flat. Tell it to get lost as the storm rolls in. But instead I light a candle for my grief, sit alongside it. A friend reaches out and we gently discuss our pain and it makes it feel lighter. Because we are all grieving, aren’t we? We all have holes in our lives. Patched up at the sides. I’m no stranger to grief. I know him well. I can face him. We can be friends. He is proof that my heart has capacity to love with so much fullness. Even for the people who once hurt me. A heart incapable of love doesn’t break. But mind has broken so many times into a million tiny pieces. And it is still there. I feel it all. The joy and the deep sadness.
comment: If grief is a signal of attachment, then what hurts isn’t only absence but having ongoing bonds with places they can’t land anymore. That mismatch feels central here.
